title: Reverse a Linked List
question: How do you reverse a singly linked list?
expected_answer: Iterate through the list, changing the next pointer of each node to point to the previous node.
hints: Consider the basics of Reverse a Linked List

title: Detect Cycle
question: How to detect a cycle in a linked list?
expected_answer: Use two pointers, slow and fast.
hints: Consider the basics of Detect Cycle

title: Binary Search
question: Implement binary search.
expected_answer: Find middle, if target < mid search left, else search right.
hints: Consider the basics of Binary Search

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
question: Merge k sorted linked lists.
expected_answer: Use a min-heap to keep track of the smallest current element.
hints: Consider the basics of Merge K Sorted Lists

title: LRU Cache
question: Design an LRU Cache.
expected_answer: Use a dict for fast lookup and a doubly linked list for fast removal.
hints: Consider the basics of LRU Cache

title: Valid Parentheses
question: Check if string of parentheses is valid.
expected_answer: Use a stack to push opening brackets and pop/check closing brackets.
hints: Consider the basics of Valid Parentheses

title: Two Sum
question: Find two numbers that add up to target.
expected_answer: Store seen numbers in a hash map.
hints: Consider the basics of Two Sum

title: Longest Substring
question: Find longest substring without repeating characters.
expected_answer: Use a sliding window and a set/dict to track seen characters.
hints: Consider the basics of Longest Substring

title: Polymorphism
question: Explain Polymorphism in OOP.
expected_answer: The ability of different objects to respond to the same method call in their own way.
hints: Consider the basics of Polymorphism

title: Inheritance vs Composition
question: Difference between inheritance and composition?
expected_answer: Inheritance is an IS-A relationship, Composition is a HAS-A relationship.
hints: Consider the basics of Inheritance vs Composition

title: Encapsulation
question: What is encapsulation?
expected_answer: Bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit or class, restricting direct access.
hints: Consider the basics of Encapsulation

title: Abstract Class vs Interface
question: Difference between Abstract Class and Interface.
expected_answer: Abstract classes can have state and implemented methods, interfaces generally define a contract.
hints: Consider the basics of Abstract Class vs Interface

title: SOLID Principles
question: What are the SOLID principles?
expected_answer: Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion.
hints: Consider the basics of SOLID Principles

title: Singleton Pattern
question: Explain the Singleton pattern and its drawbacks.
expected_answer: Ensures a class has only one instance. Drawbacks include hidden dependencies and difficulty in testing.
hints: Consider the basics of Singleton Pattern

title: Factory Pattern
question: What is the Factory Method Pattern?
expected_answer: Creates objects without specifying the exact class to create.
hints: Consider the basics of Factory Pattern

title: Method Overloading vs Overriding
question: Difference between overloading and overriding?
expected_answer: Overloading is same method name with different params (compile time). Overriding is same signature in subclass (runtime).
hints: Consider the basics of Method Overloading vs Overriding

title: ACID Properties
question: What are the ACID properties?
expected_answer: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability.
hints: Consider the basics of ACID Properties

title: Indexes
question: How do database indexes work?
expected_answer: Data structures (like B-Trees) that improve speed of data retrieval at the cost of writes and space.
hints: Consider the basics of Indexes

title: Normalization
question: What is database normalization?
expected_answer: Process of organizing data to reduce redundancy and improve data integrity (1NF, 2NF, 3NF).
hints: Consider the basics of Normalization

title: SQL vs NoSQL
question: When would you choose NoSQL over SQL?
expected_answer: When you need flexible schema, horizontal scaling for massive data, or document-based storage.
hints: Consider the basics of SQL vs NoSQL

title: Joins
question: Explain the different types of SQL JOINs.
expected_answer: INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, FULL OUTER JOIN.
sample_code: SELECT * FROM A INNER JOIN B ON A.id = B.id;
hints: Consider the basics of Joins

title: Transactions
question: What is a database transaction?
expected_answer: A logical unit of work that must either completely succeed or completely fail.
hints: Consider the basics of Transactions

title: Deadlocks
question: What is a deadlock and how to prevent it?
expected_answer: When two transactions wait for locks held by each other. Prevent by acquiring locks in a consistent order.
hints: Consider the basics of Deadlocks

title: CAP Theorem
question: Explain the CAP theorem.
expected_answer: A distributed data store can only simultaneously provide two of three: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance.
hints: Consider the basics of CAP Theorem

title: Process vs Thread
question: What is the difference between a process and a thread?
expected_answer: A process is an executing program with its own memory space. A thread is a lightweight unit of execution within a process sharing the same memory.
hints: Consider the basics of Process vs Thread

title: Virtual Memory
question: Explain virtual memory.
expected_answer: A memory management technique that gives the illusion of a very large main memory by paging to disk.
hints: Consider the basics of Virtual Memory

title: Mutex vs Semaphore
question: Difference between a mutex and a semaphore.
expected_answer: Mutex provides mutual exclusion (locking). Semaphore is a signaling mechanism (counting).
hints: Consider the basics of Mutex vs Semaphore

title: Deadlock Conditions
question: What are the four Coffman conditions for deadlock?
expected_answer: Mutual exclusion, Hold and wait, No preemption, Circular wait.
hints: Consider the basics of Deadlock Conditions

title: Context Switching
question: What happens during a context switch?
expected_answer: Saving the state of the old process/thread and loading the state of the new one.
hints: Consider the basics of Context Switching

title: Paging vs Segmentation
question: Difference between paging and segmentation?
expected_answer: Paging divides memory into fixed-size blocks. Segmentation divides memory into variable-sized logical blocks.
hints: Consider the basics of Paging vs Segmentation

title: System Calls
question: What is a system call?
expected_answer: The programmatic way a program requests a service from the kernel.
hints: Consider the basics of System Calls

title: Thrashing
question: What is thrashing?
expected_answer: When a system spends more time paging data between memory and disk than executing instructions.
hints: Consider the basics of Thrashing

title: OSI Model
question: What are the 7 layers of the OSI model?
expected_answer: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.
hints: Consider the basics of OSI Model

title: TCP vs UDP
question: Differences between TCP and UDP?
expected_answer: TCP is connection-oriented, reliable, ordered. UDP is connectionless, unreliable, fast.
hints: Consider the basics of TCP vs UDP

title: DNS
question: How does DNS work?
expected_answer: Translates human-readable domain names to IP addresses via a hierarchical lookup.
hints: Consider the basics of DNS

title: HTTP vs HTTPS
question: Difference between HTTP and HTTPS.
expected_answer: HTTPS uses TLS/SSL to encrypt HTTP requests and responses.
hints: Consider the basics of HTTP vs HTTPS

title: Three-way Handshake
question: Explain the TCP three-way handshake.
expected_answer: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK.
hints: Consider the basics of Three-way Handshake

title: IP Addresses
question: Difference between IPv4 and IPv6?
expected_answer: IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses, IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses.
hints: Consider the basics of IP Addresses

title: Subnetting
question: What is subnetting?
expected_answer: Dividing a single IP network into multiple smaller logical sub-networks.
hints: Consider the basics of Subnetting

title: BGP
question: What is BGP?
expected_answer: Border Gateway Protocol, the routing protocol that makes the internet work by routing traffic across autonomous systems.
hints: Consider the basics of BGP

title: URL Shortener
question: Design a URL shortener like bit.ly.
expected_answer: Use a highly available datastore, generate unique hashes, handle redirects.
hints: Consider the basics of URL Shortener

title: Load Balancing
question: What is a load balancer and how does it work?
expected_answer: Distributes incoming network traffic across multiple servers to ensure reliability and capacity.
hints: Consider the basics of Load Balancing

title: Caching Strategies
question: Explain different caching strategies.
expected_answer: Write-through, write-around, write-back, cache-aside.
hints: Consider the basics of Caching Strategies

title: Microservices
question: Pros and cons of Microservices vs Monolith.
expected_answer: Pros: Independent deployment, scaling, tech stacks. Cons: Complexity, network overhead, data consistency.
hints: Consider the basics of Microservices

title: Database Sharding
question: What is database sharding?
expected_answer: Splitting a database horizontally across multiple servers to distribute load and data.
hints: Consider the basics of Database Sharding

title: Message Queues
question: When would you use a message queue like Kafka or RabbitMQ?
expected_answer: To decouple services, handle asynchronous processing, and buffer spikes in traffic.
hints: Consider the basics of Message Queues

title: Rate Limiting
question: How would you design a rate limiter?
expected_answer: Use algorithms like Token Bucket, Leaky Bucket, or Sliding Window Log via Redis.
hints: Consider the basics of Rate Limiting

title: Consistent Hashing
question: Explain consistent hashing.
expected_answer: A hashing technique that minimizes the number of keys that need to be remapped when a hash table is resized.
hints: Consider the basics of Consistent Hashing

title: Conflict
question: Tell me about a time you had a conflict with a coworker.
expected_answer: STAR method: Situation, Task, Action, Result. Focus on resolution and empathy.
hints: Consider the basics of Conflict

title: Failure
question: Tell me about a time you failed.
expected_answer: Focus on what you learned and how you adapted, not just the failure itself.
hints: Consider the basics of Failure

title: Leadership
question: Tell me about a time you showed leadership.
expected_answer: Taking initiative, guiding a team, or owning a project.
hints: Consider the basics of Leadership

title: Tight Deadline
question: How do you handle tight deadlines?
expected_answer: Prioritization, communication with stakeholders, cutting non-essential scope.
hints: Consider the basics of Tight Deadline

title: Constructive Feedback
question: Tell me about a time you received difficult feedback.
expected_answer: Listening, understanding, and actionable changes made.
hints: Consider the basics of Constructive Feedback

title: Disagreement with Manager
question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager.
expected_answer: Professional discussion, data-driven arguments, and disagree-and-commit if necessary.
hints: Consider the basics of Disagreement with Manager

title: Proudest Achievement
question: What is your proudest professional achievement?
expected_answer: Highlight impact, complexity, and your specific role.
hints: Consider the basics of Proudest Achievement

title: Learning
question: Tell me about a time you had to learn a new technology quickly.
expected_answer: Reading docs, building prototypes, and applying it successfully to the project.
hints: Consider the basics of Learning
